---
title: "NextGen Empirical Finance Research Camp"
subtitle: "An intensive 8-week, research-grade introduction to empirical finance for advanced high-school students"
pagetitle: "NextGen Empirical Finance Research Camp — 8-Week Edition"
description-meta: >
  An intensive 8-week empirical finance research camp for advanced high-school students.
  Hosted by Prof. Lei Gao at George Mason University's Costello College of Business.
  Probability, OLS, causal inference, quasi-experiments, text-as-data, and a capstone
  research paper carried all the way to a conference talk and a real submission —
  built around real data and real methods.
toc: false
number-sections: false
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    css: styles.scss
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image">
          <meta name="twitter:title" content="NextGen Empirical Finance Research Camp">
          <meta name="twitter:description" content="An intensive 8-week empirical-finance research camp for advanced high-school students, hosted by Prof. Lei Gao at GMU.">
          <meta property="og:type" content="website">
          <meta property="og:title" content="NextGen Empirical Finance Research Camp — 8-Week Edition">
          <meta property="og:description" content="An intensive 8-week empirical-finance research camp for advanced high-school students, hosted by Prof. Lei Gao at GMU.">
          <meta property="og:site_name" content="NextGen Empirical Finance Research Camp">
---

::: {.hero}

::: {.hero-eyebrow}
George Mason University · Costello College of Business
:::

# NextGen Empirical Finance Research Camp {.hero-title}

### Eight intensive weeks. Real data. Real methods. A research paper, a conference talk, and a submission at the end. {.hero-subtitle}

A textbook-and-lab system for high-school researchers who want to do
empirical finance the way it is actually done: with code, with data,
and with intellectual honesty about what we can and cannot conclude.

::: {.hero-cta}
[Start here →](book/00-front-matter/00-preface.md){.btn .btn-primary}
[How to use the book](book/00-front-matter/02-how-to-use.md){.btn .btn-outline}
[View source on GitHub](https://github.com/edwardlg/nextgen-empirical-finance-camp-8wk){.btn .btn-outline}
:::

::: {.hero-host}
Hosted by **[Prof. Lei Gao](https://business.gmu.edu/)**, Associate Professor of Finance,
Costello College of Business, George Mason University.
:::

:::

::: {.quick-stats}
::: {.stat}
[**8**]{.stat-num} [Weeks]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**44**]{.stat-num} [Chapters]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**40**]{.stat-num} [Notebooks]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**34**]{.stat-num} [Data cards]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**5**]{.stat-num} [Capstones]{.stat-lbl}
:::
::: {.stat}
[**1**]{.stat-num} [Instructor manual]{.stat-lbl}
:::
:::

---

## The 8-week arc

Eight intensive weeks take you from the logic of inference to a finished,
submittable paper. Weeks 1–6 build the toolkit (probability, OLS, causal
inference, quasi-experiments, reading the canon, text/AI); Weeks 7–8 are
your own research project, carried all the way through robustness,
manuscript, conference talk, and submission. The symposium-and-paper
material that a longer camp spreads across extra weeks is folded into the
arc: second-round **robustness v2** lands in Week 4, the **manuscript build**
in Week 7, and the **conference talk, poster, defense, and submission** in
Week 8. (Deeper, optional treatments of these are available on demand in
office hours.)

::: {.week-grid}

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 1](book/weeks/week-01/README.md)
**Probability, Inference, and the Logic of Estimation**

The two laws, expectations as geometry, sampling distributions, LLN/CLT, the
mechanics of a $t$-test. Lab: simulating the *coin-flip universe*.
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 2](book/weeks/week-02/README.md)
**OLS, Geometry, and Inference**

OLS in matrix form, Gauss-Markov, Frisch-Waugh-Lovell, robust and clustered
standard errors, omitted-variable bias. Lab: Fama-MacBeth on CRSP.
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 3](book/weeks/week-03/README.md)
**Causal Inference Foundations**

Potential outcomes and SUTVA, matching and propensity scores, entropy
balancing, IV and weak-IV pathology. Lab: making weak instruments lie.
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 4](book/weeks/week-04/README.md)
**Quasi-Experiments in Finance**

Event studies, staggered DiD (TWFE vs. Callaway-Sant'Anna), regression
discontinuity, synthetic control, Bartik shift-share, plus **robustness v2**
(multiple testing, heterogeneity, mechanisms, external validity). Lab: HMDA DiD.
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 5](book/weeks/week-05/README.md)
**Reading the Empirical Finance Canon**

Reader's guides to Fama-French 1992 & 1993, Jegadeesh-Titman 1993,
Petersen 2009, Bertrand-Duflo-Mullainathan 2004. *What does a JF paper actually do?*
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 6](book/weeks/week-06/README.md)
**Text, AI, and Alternative Data**

Patent value, 10-K similarity, Loughran-McDonald sentiment, fair-lending
decomposition, and an AI copilot lab — without fooling yourself.
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 7](book/weeks/week-07/README.md)
**Your Project, Part I**

Idea generation, data acquisition, pre-analysis plan, dataset
construction, identification memo, and the **manuscript build** (abstract,
publication-grade tables/figures, intro, literature). The week you stop being a student.
:::

::: {.week-card}
### [Week 8](book/weeks/week-08/README.md)
**Your Project, Part II**

Specification curve, robustness battery, writing the paper, peer review,
defense, replication packet — plus the **conference talk, poster, and
defense** and a **real submission** (Young Scholars Journal, MARS, SSRN,
the conference circuit). Final manuscript, repo, and submission packet.
:::

:::

---

## Who this is for

This camp is for advanced high-school students with strong precalculus or
calculus, comfort with Python or strong motivation to learn it quickly,
and an appetite for the kind of math that begins:

$$
\hat{\boldsymbol{\beta}}_{\text{OLS}} = (\mathbf{X}'\mathbf{X})^{-1}\mathbf{X}'\mathbf{y}.
$$

If that equation is intimidating but interesting, you are exactly the
intended reader. Appendix A is a math toolkit; Appendix B is a setup
guide; the prerequisite self-test will tell you which gaps to close
first.

::: {.cta-row}
[Take the prerequisite self-test →](book/00-front-matter/03-prerequisite-self-test.md){.btn .btn-outline}
:::

---

## What you'll build

By the end of week 8 you will have:

- A **research paper** answering a question of your own choosing, with a
  pre-analysis plan, a real dataset, an identification strategy, a
  specification curve, and an honest robustness section (Weeks 7–8).
- A **second-round robustness battery** (Romano–Wolf multiple-testing,
  causal-forest heterogeneity, sequential-*g* mediation, transportability) —
  the robustness v2 toolkit folded into Week 4.
- A **press-ready manuscript** — 250-word abstract, publication-grade
  tables and figures, rewritten introduction, three-strand literature
  review — built in Week 7.
- A **public replication packet**: code, data references, a README, an
  environment file, and a one-command rebuild (Week 8).
- A **conference talk and poster** that survive an adversarial Q&A
  session — including the questions you cannot yet answer (Week 8).
- A **real submission**: an SSRN preprint, a Schar Young Scholars Journal
  portal submission, a GMU MARS repository deposit, and a populated
  conference-submission calendar for the year ahead (Week 8).
- **Reading fluency** in five canonical empirical-finance papers, plus
  five more from the text/AI tradition.

---

## How to use the book

::: {.use-grid}
::: {.use-card}
### For students
Read each week's chapters in order. Work the problem sets *before*
opening the solutions. Run every notebook. The labs are the part where
you actually learn.
:::

::: {.use-card}
### For instructors
The [instructor manual](book/instructor-manual/README.md) has a pacing
guide, grading rubrics, common pitfalls, and answer keys. The
[capstone gallery](book/capstones/README.md) gives five fully-specified
projects with synthetic illustrative results.
:::

::: {.use-card}
### For independent readers
You can run every notebook on **Colab** or **Binder** directly from each
notebook page — look for the buttons at the top. The
[`environment.yml`](https://github.com/edwardlg/nextgen-empirical-finance-camp-8wk/blob/main/environment.yml)
pins the conda environment.
:::

::: {.use-card}
### For everyone
Every chapter that makes a quantitative claim flags the claim with a
`[CHECK]` tag if it has not yet been verified against a primary source.
This is on purpose. Research is a process, not a posture.
:::
:::

---

## Acknowledgements

This camp is part of the **NextGen Scholars** articulation effort to make
research-grade methods accessible to high-school students who are ready
for them. The [articulation matrix](book/00-front-matter/01-articulation-matrix.md)
maps every learning objective to a downstream undergraduate or
PhD-level competency, with sources cited.

The textbook was drafted in a multi-agent workflow that pairs planning,
writing, and review agents under human supervision. See [About](about.qmd)
for the methodology and the editorial standard.

---

::: {.footer-cta}
Ready to start?

[Read the preface →](book/00-front-matter/00-preface.md){.btn .btn-primary}
[Browse the capstones →](book/capstones/README.md){.btn .btn-outline}
[Open on GitHub →](https://github.com/edwardlg/nextgen-empirical-finance-camp-8wk){.btn .btn-outline}
:::
